response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong software-craftsmanship skills and some low-latency systems and C/C++ experience, but this role is a hardware-focused Principal Performance Architect requiring SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no silicon, SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload optimization experience, and no relevant performance-architecture seniority in this domain. The mismatch between a web/application engineering background and a chip-level performance architecture role is fundamental.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec in DLang with async I/O)
Some systems-level C/C++ work on engineering tooling at National Instruments
Long, consistent engineering tenure with strong testing and software-craftsmanship discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI-accelerator experience
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Career is web/mobile/application focused, not hardware performance architecture at Principal level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware experience
No AI/deep-learning or GPU workload expertise
